title: phegopteris engleriana
intro: Phegopteris engleriana, also known as Engler's maidenhair, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Phegopteris engleriana include treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed for its medicinal properties.
